programmes.show_description: How do they put the holes in Swiss cheese? How do they restore and improve a classic battle tank? And how do robotic librarians run one of the world's biggest book collections? S8, Ep5
How do they persuade an oyster to grow a perfect pearl? How do they put the twist in classic Pennsylvania pretzels? And how do they build Venice's traditional gondolas? S8, Ep6
Ford XR8 - Australia. Mike and Elvis engage in the ultimate Aussie showdown: Ford versus Holden. Can their Ford XR8 outpace the Holden GTS at the Mount Panorama circuit? S1, Ep5
VW Kombi - Australia. Mike and Elvis find a 1970s classic VW camper in need of some mechanical TLC. Can they turn it into a long-range camper fit for the Australian wilderness? S1, Ep6
From Bodmin To Oxfordshire. Henry and Sam are in Bodmin Moor rummaging through a collection built up over 25 years. It includes amphibious craft, motorbikes, nuts and bolts. S1, Ep7
Redruth. In Redruth, Henry and mechanic Sam are confronted with sheds filled with ramshackle contents, from Harry Potter's Anglia to a model canary-yellow Lamborghini. S1, Ep8
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their unique production processes. How are objects including saxophones made? S26, Ep2
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as 3D cups and stainless-steel beer kegs made? S26, Ep3
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Amanda and Julie are tasked with restoring a toy horse that has huge sentimental value. Meanwhile, Steve and Will repair a penny arcade game. S4, Ep6
In Suffolk, Drew picks up a collection of mid-century snout sticks originally used in pig auctions. Next, Drew and Tee spot gothic treasure in a Swansea film studio. S16, Ep15
Drew and Tee head to the former East Germany, birthplace of early modern design. They visit a fair full of Bauhaus brilliance in Berlin and one of Europe's biggest flea markets. S17, Ep1
As lone wolf, Rick Fishers, teams up with a gold field legend, Ted and Lecky uncover a bounty of historic finds. Meanwhile, the Gold Gypsies embark on high risk mission. S2, Ep5
As bushland miners The Dirt Dogs encounter a possible find of a lifetime, Henri and Kellie battle brutal weather and each other. And the Gold Gypsies strike a deadly threat. S2, Ep6
Desperate for a find, the Bushmen gamble on a dangerous mining method deep below ground. In Lightning Ridge an injury causes The Bros a serious setback. S3, Ep13
The Young Guns' claim is ravaged by a vast dust storm. Meanwhile, the Boulder Boys chase old leads and the Bushmen face a disaster fifteen metres underground. S3, Ep14
Amanda and Julie are tasked with restoring a toy horse that has huge sentimental value. Meanwhile, Steve and Will repair a penny arcade game. S4, Ep6
Hampshire. Presenter Henry Cole and motorcycle restorer Sam Lovegrove scour the UK's sheds for hidden vintage automotive treasures to fix up and sell on. S1, Ep9
In The Heart Of Oxfordshire. Henry and Sam are in the heart of Oxfordshire where they find rare Minis, 1970s Rollers, and a pristine rally car - but can they get her running? S1, Ep10
Mike / Somerset. Henry and Fuzz are down in Somerset visiting Mike's farm. Will they play it safe with a motorbike or take a risk on something agricultural? S5, Ep13
Rebecca And Sue / Nottinghamshire. The pursuit of profit sees Henry and Fuzz rummaging through Rebecca and Sue's sheds near Newark. It's home to all manner of vehicles and parts. S5, Ep14
Pricey Mice In The Saleroom. Brothers sell their late mum's furniture. And a couple are overwhelmed by inherited heirlooms. But will a former lover's engagement ring sell? S5, Ep13
Cambodia Or Bust. Angus sells a woman's possessions to fund her retirement in Cambodia. And he helps a widower part with the treasures he shared with his late wife. S5, Ep14
It's All Kicking Off. Hettie, David and their 1980s Mercedes 280 SL are in Cumbria. But who will strike a decisive blow in their best-of-five auction contest? S27, Ep13
Horses Crabs And Sausages. Hettie and David hope that their 1980s Mercedes SL 280 convertible will cope with the challenging roads of the 'District' as they hunt for antiques. S27, Ep14
Sporting Trials Car. Henry and Fuzz find a rare custom IRIS Sporting Trials Car. It's irresistible for the boys who get to work returning it to its former glory. S2, Ep8
H Is For Help! With the workshop haemorrhaging money, Richard brings in help. A merchandising scheme backfires when Lewis massively over-orders stock. S5, Ep4
Bright Ideas. Guz transforms his S2000 with new lights and under glow, jumps into a turbo Micra thrill ride, and explores bold builds from across the modding world. S1, Ep7
Japanese scientists stimulate oysters to form perfect pearls. Handcrafted pretzels are made with a twist. Workers maintain the craft of the gondolier. S8, Ep7
Roulette wheels are made to be perfectly unpredictable. The secrets behind the purest olive oil are revealed. An old wreck is made into the Batmobile. S8, Ep8
The largest football stadium in the United States is turned into an ice hockey rink. Plus, robots are programmed to mass-produce cars. S8, Ep9
Over a century of Italian craftsmanship is squeezed into an accordion. Workers create callers that make duck hunters sound just like their prey. S8, Ep10
Hilux - Australia. Mike and Elvis hunt for Australia's favourite pick up, the Hilux. Despite years of farm use, the boys aim to create the ultimate off-roading beast. S1, Ep7
Holden Surf Wagon - Australia. Mike and Elvis hit the beach for the ultimate surf wagon: the iconic Holden Sandman. Can they transform a drab panel van into a hit budget version? S1, Ep8
Hampshire. Presenter Henry Cole and motorcycle restorer Sam Lovegrove scour the UK's sheds for hidden vintage automotive treasures to fix up and sell on. S1, Ep9
In The Heart Of Oxfordshire. Henry and Sam are in the heart of Oxfordshire where they find rare Minis, 1970s Rollers, and a pristine rally car - but can they get her running? S1, Ep10
More everyday items go under the microscope. How are objects including prefabricated conservatories and copper cookware made? S26, Ep4
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects including leaf and debris vacuums, canned meat and fillet knives made? S26, Ep5
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Upholsterer Sonnaz is asked to revamp a broken theatre chair. Silversmith Brenton works on a locket, while Steve repairs an unusual clock. S4, Ep7
Big changes are afoot for Drew in Conwy. He hunts for fresh stock in a stunning 1730s Sussex mansion, and in Lincoln he spots an unusual 19th century medical cabinet. S17, Ep2
Modern day treasure hunter Drew Pritchard travels the length and breadth of the UK in search of weird and wonderful objects. The bigger the hoard, the happier he is. S17, Ep3
On their remote Jackson site, the Dirt Dogs gamble their season on a risky plan, and some uninvited guests are caught red-handed. Meanwhile, Ted and Lecky uncover a huge find. S2, Ep7
Ted and Lecky unearth an ancient mystery that could lead to a hidden treasure trove as the Gold Gypsies fight for survival. Meanwhile, Henri and Kellie are running out of supplies. S2, Ep8
A summer storm sees the Young Guns race to save their only claim from a flood. And, former trucker Chris arrives in Lightning Ridge, desperate to make a fortune in opal. S3, Ep15
The Tunnel Rats get the lifeline they desperately need. Meanwhile, veteran prospector Col Duff is preparing for a week-long opal hunt far from civilisation. S3, Ep16
Upholsterer Sonnaz is asked to revamp a broken theatre chair. Silversmith Brenton works on a locket, while Steve repairs an unusual clock. S4, Ep7
West Coast Of Cornwall. Henry and Sam are on the west coast of Cornwall. Sifting through 50 years of vintage vehicles, they find the first-ever Lotus Elan and a pre-war Sunbeam. S1, Ep11
Oxfordshire. Henry and Sam are in Oxfordshire, checking out a shed full of oversized items. Henry likes a tractor in blue and Sam falls for a Midget racer in a hedge. S1, Ep12
Max / Dorset. In Bournemouth, Henry and Fuzz look through Max's vast automobilia collection. Whilst some are fully restored, others need fixing up. What will they come away with? S5, Ep15
Rob / Derbyshire. In Derbyshire, Henry and Fuzz visit Rob's haven of classic and rare bikes. They explore his collection of 1960s Lambretta scooters and 1920s 'flat tankers'. S5, Ep16
Selling Dad's Army. Angus helps two siblings clear their dad's collection of model soldiers, then helps a woman clear a collection of ornaments that threaten her health. S5, Ep15
Wheels Of Fortune. Angus travels to Scotland to meet Norma, a widow struggling to let go of her late husband's much-loved possessions as she prepares to downsize. S5, Ep16
Auction Showdown! It's the end of the tour for David Harper and Hettie Jago, as they head towards their final auction. Who will be crowned the winner? S27, Ep15
£1500 Each North. Natasha and Ishy point their classic car to Northumberland in search of profit-making collectables. Candelabra and militaria go under the gavel. S27, Ep16
Machinery Mementos. In Oxford, Angus helps two NHS workers sell 30 years' worth of collectables. Later, he discovers over 200 crane replicas, but how will they sell at auction? S3, Ep9
The Beaver & A Russian Egg. Angus uncovers expensive oak and a Fabergé egg in Herefordshire, while rare single malt whiskey means big bids and spirited surprises! S6, Ep19
Singer Russell Watson invites Angus to his amazing home, and ex-Emmerdale starlet Roxy Shahidi invites her co-star Matthew Wolfenden to auction. S5, Ep3
Workers manufacture toilets that massage and blow-dry. The Navy tests a pilotless helicopter. A jackhammer that doesn't cause the shakes is built. S8, Ep11
The secrets behind keeping the cars of Daytona on the track for 24 hours of racing are revealed. The sweetest sugarcane is turned into Caribbean rum. S8, Ep12
